title: Symplectic Embeddings of Rational Homology Balls into Projective Space
speaker: John Etnyre
abstract: I will discuss how to build small symplectic caps for contact manifolds as a step in building small closed symplectic 4-manifolds. As an application of the construction, I will give explicit handlebody descriptions of symplectic embeddings of rational homology balls into CP^2. This is joint work with Hyunki Min, Lisa Piccirillo, and Agniva Roy.

proof_stages.label: Smooth embedding obstructions for lens spaces
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins from the smooth embedding problem, explaining that a rational homology sphere embedded in R^4 or CP^2 must bound a rational homology ball on one side, and specializing this obstruction to lens spaces via Liska's classification.

proof_stages.label: Contact-type reformulation and small caps
proof_stages.summary: The smooth question is refined to contact-type hypersurface embeddings in symplectic four-manifolds, where lens spaces in CP^2 force a rational-homology-ball filling and motivate the construction of small symplectic caps with explicit handles.

proof_stages.label: Markov balls and the target theorem
proof_stages.summary: After introducing Markov triples and the rational homology balls B_{p,q}, the speaker recalls Vianna's symplectic embeddings and Evans-Smith's converse, then states the main theorem constructing an explicit b_2=1 cap whose gluing yields CP^2 with the standard symplectic structure.

proof_stages.label: Gay's concave cobordism from rational open books
proof_stages.summary: Rational open books and their supported contact structures are reviewed in order to apply Gay's theorem, which attaches two-handles to binding components and produces a symplectic cobordism with both boundary components concave, though with a potentially overtwisted upper contact structure.

proof_stages.label: Topological cap by torus-framed surgery
proof_stages.summary: A lens space is modeled as a torus interval with collapsed slopes, and torus-framed surgery on a carefully chosen torus knot splits the upper boundary as a connected sum of the two other lens spaces associated to the Markov triple, giving the desired cap topologically.

proof_stages.label: Symplecticity of the surgery cobordism
proof_stages.summary: Because the chosen positive torus knot is the binding of a rational open book supporting the universally tight lens-space contact structure, Gay's theorem makes the cobordism symplectic, reducing the remaining issue to identifying the upper contact structure.

proof_stages.label: Nonloose torus-knot surgery identifies the boundary
proof_stages.summary: Using the classification of nonloose torus knots via tight solid tori and Farey-graph data, a specific surgery theorem shows that the upper boundary is exactly the connected sum of universally tight contact structures, allowing the rational homology balls to glue symplectically.

proof_stages.label: Kirby-calculus induction to standard CP^2
proof_stages.summary: The resulting symplectic manifold is shown smoothly to be CP^2 by an explicit handle picture whose slides realize Markov mutations, reducing inductively to the base case X_{1,1,1}; the almost-toric interpretation of the mutations is revisited in the Q&A.

examples.engagement_reason: He applies the Markov-number obstruction to L(9,5), identifying it as the boundary of B(3,2) and concluding it cannot embed as a contact-type hypersurface.
